body: Closes https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/4684 Here the changes now expose `ibg` to active cells (which includes partial cells), and then using `ᵃ`-type difference operators for the case then the cells are not at the immersed boundaries. Applying this fix now to the MWE in https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/4684 we get which is the expected behavior. Also note that this fix addresses the MWE in https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/4634 for the flat bottom case where there is a row of flat bottomed-partial cells, but does not fully solve the problem as divergence issues persists with other nontrivial bottom topography such as a linear slope. @glwagner @simone-silvestri

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

title: Make nested functions in conditional differences immersed metric-aware
